game_info.title: Cannon Shot
game_info.concept: **Cannon Shot:** Players complete physics-based puzzles by guiding a fixed number of balls from a cannon into designated buckets. To play, players drag and drop or rotate pre-placed environmental objects like ramps and deflectors on the screen using touch gestures, strategically altering the balls' trajectory. Once objects are positioned, players tap the cannon to fire a set quantity of balls, typically 10-20, which then fall under gravity and interact with the environment. A level is won when all 2-5 buckets are filled with at least one ball, and lost if the player runs out of balls before achieving this goal. Completing levels sequentially unlocks new, more complex puzzle layouts and occasionally new cannon skins, with difficulty scaling through increased object interaction complexity and stricter ball count limits.
game_info.description: Guide cannon balls into buckets by strategically positioning ramps and deflectors! Each level gives you a limited number of balls to fill all buckets. Position the movable objects carefully, then fire the cannon to watch physics do its magic. Fill every bucket with at least one ball to win and unlock the next level. Run out of balls before completing the objective, and you'll need to try again!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←→): Select/move between objects
Space: Pick up/drop object (toggle grab mode)
A/D or Arrow Up/Down: Rotate grabbed object
Enter: Start game from menu
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart from game over screen
Z: Fire cannon (when in playing phase and not editing)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic object manipulation and cannon firing mechanics. This validates that players can select, move, and rotate objects, and that the cannon fires balls correctly with proper physics simulation.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically cycle through all movable objects, perform rotations on each, then fire the cannon multiple times to ensure balls spawn correctly and interact with the environment under gravity.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All objects should be selectable and rotatable. Cannon should fire balls that fall under gravity and collide with objects and boundaries. Physics simulation should remain stable without errors or unexpected behavior.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by strategically positioning objects to guide balls into buckets. This validates the victory detection system and level progression mechanics.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Position ramps and deflectors in optimal configurations to channel balls directly into all buckets, then fire the cannon. The system will automatically arrange objects to ensure all buckets receive balls.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: When all buckets contain at least one ball, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state, display victory message with score, and allow restart. Level completion should be properly detected.

TEST_2.expected: When all buckets contain at least one ball, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state, display victory message with score, and allow restart. Level completion should be properly detected.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition by deliberately wasting all balls without filling buckets. This validates the failure detection system and ensures proper game state transitions.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Position objects away from buckets, then fire all available balls so they miss all targets and fall off screen or settle without entering buckets.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When all balls are used without filling all buckets, game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state, display appropriate message, and allow restart via R key.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing pause and unpause functionality during active gameplay. This validates that ESC key properly freezes and resumes game state without causing physics glitches.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Start gameplay, fire some balls, then repeatedly pause and unpause while balls are in motion to ensure physics engine handles state changes correctly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Pausing should freeze all physics and display pause overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. No balls should clip through objects or behave unexpectedly after pause/unpause cycles.
TEST_4.description: Testing pause and unpause functionality during active gameplay. This validates that ESC key properly freezes and resumes game state without causing physics glitches.
TEST_4.strategy: Start gameplay, fire some balls, then repeatedly pause and unpause while balls are in motion to ensure physics engine handles state changes correctly.
TEST_4.expected: Pausing should freeze all physics and display pause overlay. Unpausing should resume exactly where it left off. No balls should clip through objects or behave unexpectedly after pause/unpause cycles.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing object collision and physics interactions. This validates that balls properly bounce off ramps, deflectors, and walls with realistic physics response.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Position objects at various angles and locations, fire balls to hit them at different velocities and angles, observing bounce behavior and trajectory changes.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Balls should bounce realistically off all surfaces with appropriate restitution. Rotated objects should deflect balls according to their angle. No balls should pass through solid objects or get stuck.
TEST_5.description: Testing object collision and physics interactions. This validates that balls properly bounce off ramps, deflectors, and walls with realistic physics response.
TEST_5.strategy: Position objects at various angles and locations, fire balls to hit them at different velocities and angles, observing bounce behavior and trajectory changes.
TEST_5.expected: Balls should bounce realistically off all surfaces with appropriate restitution. Rotated objects should deflect balls according to their angle. No balls should pass through solid objects or get stuck.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing edge cases with boundary conditions like balls near screen edges, buckets at capacity, and rapid cannon firing.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Fire balls rapidly in succession, attempt to overfill buckets, and position objects near screen boundaries to test edge collision detection and bucket fill logic.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Buckets should properly detect balls entering them regardless of entry angle or speed. Screen boundaries should contain all objects. Rapid firing should maintain stable frame rate and physics accuracy.
TEST_6.description: Testing edge cases with boundary conditions like balls near screen edges, buckets at capacity, and rapid cannon firing.
TEST_6.strategy: Fire balls rapidly in succession, attempt to overfill buckets, and position objects near screen boundaries to test edge collision detection and bucket fill logic.
TEST_6.expected: Buckets should properly detect balls entering them regardless of entry angle or speed. Screen boundaries should contain all objects. Rapid firing should maintain stable frame rate and physics accuracy.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing full game loop from start screen through multiple levels. This validates complete user journey including restart functionality and state persistence.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Complete level 1, progress to level 2, intentionally lose, restart with R key, then win again to verify all state transitions and resets work correctly throughout the entire game flow.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Game should smoothly transition through all phases: START → PLAYING → GAME_OVER → START. All game state variables should reset properly. Levels should increase in difficulty and display correct information.
TEST_7.description: Testing full game loop from start screen through multiple levels. This validates complete user journey including restart functionality and state persistence.
